title: Never Split the Difference
subtitle: Negotiating As If Your Life Depended On It
author: Chris Voss (with Tahl Raz)
themes: Tactical empathy as a strategic tool
The power of 'No' to create safety and momentum
Calibrated questions to give the illusion of control
Mirroring builds rapport and uncovers information
Labeling emotions defuses tension and builds trust
The Ackerman model for structured bargaining
Black Swans as hidden leverage points
Never splitting the difference as a rejection of lazy compromise
excerpt: A former FBI hostage negotiator reveals the counterintuitive negotiation techniques that work when the stakes are life and death — and shows how to apply them in business and everyday life. The core thesis: negotiation is not a rational, logic-based process. It is an emotional game where tactical empathy, active listening, and the strategic use of 'No' produce better outcomes than traditional win-win compromise.
